questions.q: If the following represent the two lines with real point of intersection then $\alpha_1 x+\alpha_2 y=k_1$, $\beta_1 x+\beta_2 y=k_2$. Also, $\left(\alpha_1, \alpha_2\right),\left(\beta_1, \beta_2\right)$ lie on $x^2+y^2=1$ then
options: lines can represent asymptotes of rectangular hyperbola

Normal hyperbola at P intersects the x -axis at N and the $y$-axis at $\mathrm{N}^{\prime}$. questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If a hyperbola whose foci are $(-2,4)$ and $(4,6)$ touches $y$-axis then equation of hyperbola is
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & b^2=4 \times 2=8,2 a e=\sqrt{40} \\ & a^2 e^2=10=b^2+a^2\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow a^2=2 \\ & \text { Centre }=(1,5) \\ & \text { Equation of transverse axis } \equiv x-3 y+14=0\end{aligned}$
Equation of conjugate axis $\equiv 3 x+y-8=0$
Equation of hyperbola $=\frac{(3 x+y-8)^2}{1}-\frac{(x-3 y+14)^2}{4}=20$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
